data.title: VanEck Extends Bitcoin ETF Fee Waiver to 2026
data.description: According to Odaily, U.S. asset management firm VanEck has revealed that its total assets under management have reached $171.7 billion. The company's digital asset management now exceeds $5.2 billion, covering its entire range of digital asset solutions. Additionally, VanEck announced an extension of the fee waiver period for its Bitcoin ETF, moving the deadline from January 10, 2026, to July 31, 2026.

data.title: Concerns Raised Over Potential Federal Reserve Chair Appointment
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, insiders from Wall Street and the U.S. business community are expressing concerns to U.S. President Donald Trump regarding the potential appointment of Kevin Hassett as Federal Reserve Chair. The apprehensions stem from Hassett's political role as Director of the National Economic Council and his perceived lack of credibility among Federal Reserve staff and market participants. The business community emphasizes the importance of the Federal Reserve's independence, fearing that Hassett's appointment could lead to increased long-term interest rates and internal chaos within the institution.

If Hassett were to lower short-term rates through a divided vote amidst persistent inflation pressures, as Trump desires, it could be seen as political interference, potentially exacerbating inflation. Mortgage and consumer rates, which are benchmarked against the 10-year Treasury bond, might surge due to inflation concerns, potentially slowing the economy as midterm elections approach. Despite these warnings, Trump may choose to proceed with his decision. Other candidates for the Federal Reserve Chair position include Kevin Warsh and Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller, both considered potential contenders.

data.title: U.S. Debt Growth Will Drive Crypto's Gains, BlackRock Says in Report on AI
data.description:
According to a recent report from BlackRock focused on the implications of artificial intelligence, the ongoing growth of U.S. debt is expected to play a significant role in driving the gains of cryptocurrencies. The report suggests that as the national debt increases, it may create a favorable environment for digital currencies, attracting more investors and boosting their value in the market.
source: https://www.coindesk.com/business/2025/12/03/u-s-debt-growth-will-drive-crypto-s-gains-blackrock-says-in-report-on-ai

data.title: U.S. Bitcoin Company Sees Stock Increase After Previous Decline
data.description: According to Odaily, U.S. Bitcoin Company (ABTC), founded by U.S. President Donald Trump's son Eric Trump, experienced a 5.5% rise in early trading on Wednesday. This follows a significant drop of 38.8% in the previous session. Meanwhile, Trump Media & Technology Group saw a slight decrease, despite a brief 1.3% increase earlier in the day.

data.title: Fin Secures $17 Million in Funding Led by Pantera Capital
data.description:
According to Odaily, stablecoin cross-border remittance company Fin has announced the completion of a $17 million funding round. The investment was led by Pantera Capital, with participation from Sequoia and Samsung Next. The company has not disclosed the valuation for this round of funding. Fin has developed an application that facilitates instant cross-border remittances, including large transactions, aiming to address remittance challenges faced by users worldwide
source: https://www.binance.com/en/square/post/33221335730514?utm_source=BinanceNewsRSS

data.title: UK Officially Classifies Crypto as Property in New Digital Assets Act – Here Is Why This Changes Everything
data.description:
The United Kingdom has officially recognized cryptocurrencies as property through the introduction of a new legislative framework known as the Digital Assets Act. This groundbreaking decision is poised to significantly alter the landscape of cryptocurrency regulation and investment. Here are several reasons why this classification is a game-changer, impacting everything from the legal rights of crypto holders to the way digital assets are treated in financial transactions, taxation, and even inheritance laws. By establishing cryptocurrencies as property, the UK government not only provides a clearer legal framework for their use and ownership but also fosters a more secure environment for both investors and businesses operating in the crypto space. This move may lead to increased institutional adoption, enhanced consumer protections, and a more robust ecosystem that could ultimately drive innovation and growth in the digital asset sector.
source: https://blocknews.com/uk-officially-classifies-crypto-as-property-in-new-digital-assets-act-here-is-why-this-changes-everything/

data.title: Cryptocurrency Market Structure Bill Nears Approval, Says SEC Chair
data.description: According to Odaily, U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Chair Paul Atkins stated in an interview with Fox News that the Cryptocurrency Market Structure Bill is on the verge of being passed. If approved, the bill is expected to provide much-needed regulatory clarity for the cryptocurrency industry.

data.title: U.S. Urged to Accelerate Tokenization and AI Efforts, Says BlackRock CEO
data.description: According to Odaily, BlackRock CEO Larry Fink has emphasized the need for the United States to expedite its efforts in tokenization and artificial intelligence. Fink believes that the digitization of assets will help reduce friction within the financial system. He also noted that the U.S. is lagging behind countries like India and Brazil in the digital economy.
